title: New Smart Drugs Offer Hope in the Global Fight Against Cancer
subtitle: Recent medical studies reveal breakthrough treatments that help the body fight cancer more effectively.

summary.en: Medical researchers have shared exciting new findings at a major conference in Chicago. Scientists have developed 'smart' drugs that stop cancer cells from hiding from the immune system. One new pill has shown the ability to double survival times for patients with pancreatic cancer. These treatments offer hope to patients who previously had few options left. Experts believe these discoveries represent a significant step forward in cancer care.
summary.zh: 醫學研究人員在芝加哥的一場大型會議上分享了令人振奮的新發現。科學家開發出「智慧」藥物，能阻止癌細胞躲避免疫系統。其中一款新藥丸展現了將胰臟癌病患存活時間翻倍的能力。這些治療為先前幾乎無計可施的病患提供了希望。專家認為，這些發現代表癌症照護邁出了重大的一步。

paragraphs.en: Medical experts recently gathered in Chicago to share major developments in the fight against cancer. Building on recent progress, researchers presented new 'smart' drugs that help the body’s immune system find and destroy cancer cells that were previously hidden. These findings provide fresh hope for patients, especially those who have not responded to traditional treatments.
paragraphs.zh: 醫學專家近期齊聚芝加哥，分享對抗癌症的重大進展。基於近期的研究成果，研究人員發表了新型「智慧」藥物，能協助人體免疫系統發現並摧毀先前隱匿的癌細胞。這些發現為病患帶來了新的希望，特別是那些對傳統治療沒有反應的患者。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian, immunotherapy has changed how doctors treat cancer over the last ten years. Immunotherapy drugs work by helping the body’s own immune system attack tumours. However, these treatments do not work for every patient because some cancer cells are able to hide. Researchers have now developed a new experimental tablet called GRWD5769. This drug acts like a key, removing the 'invisibility cloaks' that allow cancer cells to stay hidden. Once these cells are exposed, the immune system can detect and destroy them.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，免疫療法在過去十年改變了醫師治療癌症的方式。免疫療法藥物的作用機制是協助人體自身的免疫系統攻擊腫瘤。然而，這些治療並非對每位病患都有效，因為部分癌細胞具備隱藏能力。研究人員現已開發出一種名為 GRWD5769 的實驗性藥錠。這種藥物就像一把鑰匙，能移除讓癌細胞得以隱身的「隱形斗篷」。一旦這些細胞暴露出來，免疫系統便能偵測並將其摧毀。

paragraphs.en: In a study involving patients from the UK, France, Spain, and Australia, researchers found that this new tablet helped shrink tumours in several common types of cancer, including lung, bowel, and liver cancer. Prof. Fiona Thistlethwaite, the lead researcher for the trial, told The Guardian that the results are very impressive. She noted that while the research is still in its early stages, this new mechanism clearly helps immunotherapy work more effectively for patients who had no other options left.
paragraphs.zh: 在一項涵蓋英國、法國、西班牙與澳洲病患的研究中，研究人員發現這種新型藥錠有助於縮小多種常見癌症的腫瘤，包括肺癌、腸癌與肝癌。該試驗的首席研究員費歐娜·希斯索斯韋特教授（Prof. Fiona Thistlethwaite）向《衛報》表示，這些結果令人印象深刻。她指出，儘管研究仍處於早期階段，但這種新機制顯然能讓免疫療法對那些已無其他選擇的病患發揮更有效的治療作用。

paragraphs.en: Another major breakthrough presented at the conference involves the world’s deadliest common cancer: pancreatic cancer. For many years, there have been very few successful treatments for this disease. However, a new pill called daraxonrasib has shown the potential to change this. In a trial of 500 patients, those who took the pill lived for an average of 13.2 months, compared to about 6.6 months for those who received standard chemotherapy. Chemotherapy is a common treatment that uses strong chemicals to kill cancer cells, though it often causes difficult side effects. The new pill not only doubled survival time but also resulted in fewer side effects for patients.
paragraphs.zh: 在會議上發表的另一項重大突破，涉及全球最致命的常見癌症：胰臟癌。多年來，針對此疾病的成功治療方法寥寥無幾。然而，一種名為 daraxonrasib 的新藥丸展現了改變現狀的潛力。在一項五百人的試驗中，服用該藥丸的病患平均存活期為十三點二個月，而接受標準化學治療的病患僅約六點六個月。化學治療是一種利用強效化學物質殺死癌細胞的常見療法，但往往會造成嚴重的副作用。這款新藥丸不僅使存活時間翻倍，且對病患造成的副作用也較少。

paragraphs.en: Dr. Rachna Shroff, an expert in gastrointestinal cancers at the University of Arizona Cancer Center, described these results as 'landscape-changing.' She noted that this discovery is one of the most significant breakthroughs in decades for patients facing this difficult diagnosis. Many patients who participated in these trials described the new therapies as giving them a 'new lease of life.'
paragraphs.zh: 亞利桑那大學癌症中心（University of Arizona Cancer Center）的腸胃道癌症專家拉赫娜·史洛夫醫師（Dr. Rachna Shroff）將這些結果形容為「改變格局」。她指出，這項發現是數十年來面對此艱難診斷的病患所迎來的最重大突破之一。許多參與這些試驗的病患形容，這些新療法給了他們「重獲新生」的機會。

paragraphs.en: Beyond these specific drugs, other researchers are exploring different ways to fight the disease. One study presented at the conference showed that combining a smart drug called ivonescimab with chemotherapy helped patients with lung cancer live 15% longer on average. Another drug, known as ozekibart, mimics a natural protein in the body to bind to cancer cells. This process triggers the death of the cancer cells while causing minimal harm to healthy tissue nearby. Cancer Research UK described the development of these new drugs as a very promising step forward for global health.
paragraphs.zh: 除了這些特定藥物外，其他研究人員也在探索對抗該疾病的不同途徑。會議中發表的一項研究顯示，將一種名為 ivonescimab 的智慧藥物與化學治療結合，能使肺癌病患的平均存活期延長百分之十五。另一種稱為 ozekibart 的藥物，則能模擬人體內的天然蛋白質以結合癌細胞。此過程會觸發癌細胞死亡，同時對周圍健康組織造成的傷害降至最低。英國癌症研究協會（Cancer Research UK）形容這些新藥的開發，是全球健康領域邁出極具前景的一步。

paragraphs.en: While these results are encouraging, experts emphasize that further studies are necessary to confirm the long-term safety and effectiveness of these treatments. The medical community remains focused on moving these experimental drugs from the laboratory to regular clinical use. For now, these findings represent a significant shift in how doctors approach cancer, moving toward more targeted and less harmful treatments. As research continues, the goal remains to turn these early successes into standard care that can help patients around the world live longer, healthier lives.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管這些結果令人振奮，專家仍強調必須進行更多研究，以確認這些療法的長期安全性與有效性。醫學界持續致力於將這些實驗性藥物從實驗室推向常規臨床應用。目前，這些發現代表醫師處理癌症的方式出現了重大轉變，正朝向更精準且傷害更小的治療邁進。隨著研究持續進行，目標依然是將這些早期的成功轉化為標準照護，協助全球病患活得更長久、更健康。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Using previous work or achievements as a base for further development.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 基於……之上；利用……作為基礎。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於實體建築，這裡指抽象的進展。文中：Building on recent progress, researchers presented new 'smart' drugs that help the body’s immune system find and destroy cancer cells that were previously hidden.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To perform a specific function or have a particular effect.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（行為）或動詞（表演），這裡指藥物的功能。文中：This drug acts like a key, removing the 'invisibility cloaks' that allow cancer cells to stay hidden.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Having a major impact that completely alters the current situation or field.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 改變局勢的；具有開創性的。
keyTerms.noteZh: 由名詞 landscape（風景）組成的複合形容詞，形容影響深遠。文中：Dr. Rachna Shroff, an expert in gastrointestinal cancers at the University of Arizona Cancer Center, described these results as 'landscape-changing.'

keyTerms.definitionEn: A period of time during which someone is able to live or be active again after being ill or near death.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 重獲新生；獲得新的生命力。
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面意思為「租約」，這裡指獲得第二次生命的機會。文中：Many patients who participated in these trials described the new therapies as giving them a 'new lease of life.'

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Removing ‘invisibility cloaks’ and safely skipping chemo: new weapons in war on cancer shared at US conference

previousArticles.title: New Medical Breakthroughs Offer Fresh Hope in Cancer Fight
previousArticles.summaryEn: Medical researchers have recently shared exciting updates on new cancer treatments at a major conference in Chicago. These new drugs work by helping the immune system find and destroy cancer cells that were previously hidden. One specific pill has shown the ability to double survival times for patients with pancreatic cancer. Another treatment helps patients with various common cancers by removing the 'invisibility cloaks' that tumors use to hide. While these results are early, experts believe they represent a significant step forward in oncology.

previousArticles.title: New Advances Bring Hope in the Fight Against Cancer
previousArticles.summaryEn: Recent medical conferences and studies have revealed major progress in cancer treatment. Scientists have developed new smart drugs that help the immune system better identify and destroy cancer cells. A new pill for pancreatic cancer has shown the ability to double survival times for patients. Surgical research suggests that choosing the right method can significantly lower the risk of cervical cancer returning. However, experts warn that a global shortage of healthcare workers could create future challenges for cancer care.

previousArticles.title: New Medical Breakthroughs Offer Hope for Cancer Patients
previousArticles.summaryEn: Medical researchers have recently shared exciting findings at a major international cancer conference. A new DNA test for breast cancer patients could help millions avoid unnecessary chemotherapy. Meanwhile, a new 'smart' injection has shown the ability to shrink or even remove tumors in patients with head, neck, and lung cancers. Another study suggests that combining new drugs with existing treatments may improve survival rates for lung cancer patients. These developments represent a significant shift in how doctors approach cancer care. Experts believe these findings could change medical practice and improve the quality of life for many people.

quiz.question: According to the article, how does the experimental tablet GRWD5769 assist in the treatment of cancer?

options.A: It directly kills cancer cells by using strong chemicals.
options.B: It exposes hidden cancer cells so the immune system can identify them.
options.C: It acts as a substitute for chemotherapy to reduce side effects.
options.D: It mimics a natural protein to bind directly to healthy tissues.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，GRWD5769 的作用如同鑰匙，移除癌細胞的「隱形斗篷」，使其暴露出來，讓免疫系統得以偵測並消滅它們。選項 A 描述的是化學治療（chemotherapy）；選項 C 雖然提到減少副作用，但並非該藥物的運作機制；選項 D 描述的是另一種藥物 ozekibart 的功能，故皆不正確。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the patients who participated in the trials for the new pancreatic cancer drug, daraxonrasib?

options.A: They had already exhausted all other available treatment options.
options.B: They experienced significantly more side effects than those on chemotherapy.
options.C: They generally had a better quality of life and longer survival compared to the control group.
options.D: They were exclusively from the United Kingdom and Australia.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第四段，使用 daraxonrasib 的患者存活時間翻倍（從 6.6 個月增至 13.2 個月），且副作用較少，這暗示了他們擁有更好的生活品質與存活率。選項 A 雖然在第三段提到其他藥物時有提及，但並未直接說明胰臟癌患者的情況；選項 B 與文中「fewer side effects」矛盾；選項 D 僅提到該國籍是針對 GRWD5769 的研究，而非 daraxonrasib。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does Dr. Rachna Shroff mean when she describes the results as 'landscape-changing'?

options.A: The results have completely altered the physical environment of the cancer center.
options.B: The results have fundamentally transformed the existing standards of cancer treatment.
options.C: The results have made it easier for doctors to map the growth of tumors.
options.D: The results have caused a temporary shift in the focus of medical research.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在此語境下，「landscape-changing」是一個隱喻，用來形容某項發現具有革命性，徹底改變了該領域（癌症治療）的現狀或範式。選項 A 將其誤解為地理或物理環境的改變；選項 C 誤將「landscape」解讀為地圖或影像；選項 D 將其視為短暫的改變，與「significant breakthrough」的語氣不符。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of this article?

options.A: Chemotherapy is no longer a viable option for treating most types of cancer.
options.B: Medical experts are shifting their focus entirely away from laboratory research.
options.C: New experimental drugs are showing potential to make cancer treatment more targeted and effective.
options.D: The fight against pancreatic cancer has been officially won by recent scientific breakthroughs.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章整體旨在介紹癌症治療的最新進展，強調「智慧型藥物」與免疫療法的結合，使治療變得更精準且副作用更少。選項 A 過度推論，文中並未否定化療的地位；選項 B 與文中提到「moving these experimental drugs from the laboratory」的目標不符；選項 D 太過誇大，文中僅提到是「promising step」與「potential」，尚未宣稱完全治癒。
